currentFocus: Upstream middleware instrumentation — logging token_spent, response_status, completion markers, and elapsed time (first token latency) at the Anthropic API boundary in real time
Building atomic checkpoint/completion detection via response_metadata.json and end-marker logging to stdout — deterministic completion tracking without downstream correlation guessing
frustrations: Correlation/pattern-matching tasks are a dead-end — he created 5 forensic queries (SQL metrics, RPC cross-ref, misclassification analysis, failure correlation) and vetoed all 5 because they're speculative noise, not root cause
Post-hoc query analysis drowns signal; he needs deterministic detection at request time, not manual inbox forensics mining through execution logs
excitements: He's cracked the architecture: upstream middleware (token + status + completion marker logged per call) beats all downstream correlation queries cold — this is the mental model breakthrough
Tiny, zero-friction utility tasks (timestamp loggers, response metadata snapshots, end-marker detectors) compound into deterministic instrumentation without the guesswork tax
inferred.nextSuggestion: Ship a single `api_response_lifecycle_log` table + middleware hook that atomically logs (call_id, token_spent, response_status, first_token_ms, completion_marker) for every Anthropic request, then give him a 3-line query to spot truncated/failed requests — kills correlation dead-end cold.
